security_gap.gap_description: The incentive claiming process is dependent on an external off-chain service for proof generation.

actor_risk.description: Users are reliant on the availability of the off-chain service to claim their rewards.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Disruption of the off-chain service prevents users from claiming earned incentives, impacting availability of rewards.

assumptions: The off-chain service providing claim proofs is essential for the claiming process.
There is no on-chain fallback or sufficient redundancy for the proof generation/distribution.

mitigation.strategy: Implement high availability and resilience for the off-chain proof service, or explore on-chain alternatives.

description: An attacker disrupts the off-chain service generating claim proofs, preventing users from claiming incentive tokens earned through boosting validators.

trace_source.matched_capability: backend service that manages the distribution and claiming of these tokens, updates proofs of the user's entitlement to Incentive tokens

security_gap.gap_description: The governance proposal queue and process can be overwhelmed by spam.

actor_risk.description: An actor possessing the minimum 10,000 $BGT required to create proposals.

behavioral_risk.rationale: An attacker with sufficient $BGT can spam proposal creation, disrupting legitimate governance activities.

assumptions: An attacker can acquire the minimum 10,000 $BGT required to create proposals.
The gas cost of proposal creation is not a prohibitive barrier for an attacker.
The governance process lacks sufficient rate limiting or spam mitigation beyond the $BGT threshold.

mitigation.strategy: Increase the $BGT threshold for proposal creation or implement other anti-spam measures (e.g., reputation, quadratic costs).

description: An attacker creates a large number of low-quality or irrelevant governance proposals to flood the system, making it difficult for the community to find and process legitimate proposals.

attack_surface.data_description: User incentive eligibility data stored off-chain

security_gap.gap_description: Sensitive user earning information is processed and stored by an off-chain service before being claimed publicly on-chain.

actor_risk.description: Users whose private earning data is handled by the off-chain service.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Compromise of the off-chain service could expose user-specific data linking addresses to boosting activity and earned, unclaimed incentives.

assumptions: The off-chain proof generation service stores user earning data (e.g., addresses, earned amounts).
The off-chain service is susceptible to unauthorized access or data breaches.

mitigation.strategy: Enhance security measures for the off-chain service, minimize data retention, encrypt sensitive data, or move eligibility calculation fully on-chain.

description: A data breach or compromise of the off-chain service that generates incentive claim proofs could lead to unauthorized disclosure of user data related to boosting activities and earned, unclaimed incentive tokens.

trace_source.matched_capability: backend service that manages the distribution and claiming of these tokens, updates proofs of the user's entitlement to Incentive tokens
